@tbl-incidence-density-rates presents the incidence density rates for the most important healthcare-associated infections (HAI) in your neonatal department. Incidence density rates are calculated per 1,000 patient days, allowing for meaningful comparison between departments with different patient volumes and lengths of stay. The primary sepsis/BSI category includes both clinical sepsis and laboratory-confirmed bloodstream infections (LCBSI); the infectious agent detection rate table reflects only cases where diagnostic cultures identified an infectious agent. Regular monitoring of these rates enables you to identify trends, evaluate the effectiveness of infection prevention measures, and compare your performance against reference data from other participating departments.
